content.text:
1	# LedgerMint - Digital Collectibles Exchange
2	
3	A full-stack platform for listing, buying, and managing digital collectibles (NFTs). Features role-based access control, order state machine with idempotency, real-time notifications, analytics dashboards, A/B testing, and anomaly detection.
4	
5	## Architecture
6	
7	| Layer | Technology |
8	|-------|-----------|
9	| Frontend | React 18, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Vite, React Query, Zustand, Recharts |
10	| Backend | Go 1.22, Echo v4, PostgreSQL 16, JWT auth, AES-256-GCM encryption |
11	| Database | PostgreSQL 16 with advisory locks, CIDR-based IP filtering |
12	| Infrastructure | Docker Compose, Nginx reverse proxy |
13	
14	## Quick Start
15	
16	```bash
17	docker compose up
18	```
19	
20	This single command builds and starts all three services (PostgreSQL, backend API, frontend).
21	
22	## Service URLs and Ports
23	
24	| Service | URL | Port |
25	|---------|-----|------|
26	| Frontend (UI) | http://localhost | 80 |
27	| Backend (API) | http://localhost:8080 | 8080 |
28	| PostgreSQL | localhost | 5432 |
29	
30	The frontend Nginx proxy forwards `/api/*` requests to the backend, so all API calls work through http://localhost/api/ as well.
31	
32	## First-Time Setup
33	
34	No default admin credentials are included in the database migrations. On first deployment, you must bootstrap an administrator account:
35	
36	```bash
37	# 1. Start services
38	docker compose up
39	
40	# 2. Create the initial administrator (interactive)
41	make create-admin
42	
43	# Or use the API directly:
44	curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/api/setup/admin \
45	  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
46	  -d '{"username":"admin","password":"<STRONG-PASSWORD>","display_name":"System Administrator"}'
47	```
48	
49	The bootstrap endpoint (`POST /api/setup/admin`) is only available when no administrator account exists. It is intentionally CSRF-exempt because it is called before any user session exists and is a one-time bootstrap operation — once an admin account is created, the endpoint returns `409 Conflict` and cannot be used again. All other API endpoints return `503 Service Unavailable` until setup is complete. You can check setup status at any time via `GET /api/setup/status`.
50	
51	> **Security:** Never use predictable passwords (e.g. `admin123`, `password`) in production. Rotate credentials immediately after deployment. Default credentials must never be committed to version control.
52	
53	## Development Seed Data
54	
55	For local development, run `make seed` after startup to populate sample users and collectibles. **These credentials are for development only and must never be used in production.**
56	
57	```bash
58	docker compose exec postgres psql -U ledgermint -d ledgermint -f /dev/stdin < scripts/seed.sql
59	```
60	
61	The seed script creates an admin and sample users with weak passwords suitable only for local testing.
62	
63	## Verification
64	
65	1. Open http://localhost in a browser
66	2. Complete the initial setup (create an admin account) or run `make seed` for development
67	3. Log in with the admin credentials you created
68	4. You should see the Dashboard with stats cards (Open Orders, Unread Notifications, Roles)
69	5. Navigate to **Catalog** - after seeding, three sample collectibles appear
70	6. Navigate to **Users** (admin sidebar) to manage users and roles
71	7. Navigate to **Analytics** to see funnel, retention, and content performance charts
72	
73	## API Endpoints
74	
75	### Setup
76	- `GET /api/setup/status` - Check whether initial setup is complete (public)
77	- `POST /api/setup/admin` - Bootstrap the first administrator account (public, disabled after first admin created)
78	
79	### Authentication
80	- `POST /api/auth/login` - Login (public, rate limited)
81	- `POST /api/auth/refresh` - Refresh tokens (public)
82	- `POST /api/auth/logout` - Logout (authenticated)
83	
84	### Dashboard
85	- `GET /api/dashboard` - User dashboard stats
86	
87	### Users (administrator only)
88	- `POST /api/users` - Create user
89	- `GET /api/users` - List users (paginated)
90	- `GET /api/users/:id` - Get user
91	- `PATCH /api/users/:id` - Update user
92	- `POST /api/users/:id/roles` - Add role
93	- `DELETE /api/users/:id/roles/:roleId` - Remove role
94	- `POST /api/users/:id/unlock` - Unlock account
95	
96	### Collectibles
97	- `GET /api/collectibles` - List published collectibles (paginated)
98	- `GET /api/collectibles/mine` - List seller's own collectibles (seller)
99	- `GET /api/collectibles/:id` - Get collectible with transaction history
100	- `POST /api/collectibles` - Create listing (seller, rate limited)
101	- `PATCH /api/collectibles/:id` - Update listing (seller)
102	- `PATCH /api/collectibles/:id/hide` - Hide listing (administrator)
103	- `PATCH /api/collectibles/:id/publish` - Publish listing (administrator)
104	
105	### Orders
106	- `POST /api/orders` - Create order (buyer, requires Idempotency-Key header)
107	- `GET /api/orders` - List orders (paginated, `?role=buyer|seller`)
108	- `GET /api/orders/:id` - Get order
109	- `POST /api/orders/:id/confirm` - Confirm order (seller)
110	- `POST /api/orders/:id/process` - Start processing (seller)
111	- `POST /api/orders/:id/complete` - Complete order (seller)
112	- `POST /api/orders/:id/cancel` - Cancel order (buyer or seller)
113	- `PATCH /api/orders/:id/fulfillment` - Update fulfillment tracking (seller)
114	
115	### Messages
116	- `GET /api/orders/:orderId/messages` - List messages (paginated)
117	- `POST /api/orders/:orderId/messages` - Send message with optional attachment (multipart, 10MB limit)
118	
119	### Notifications
120	- `GET /api/notifications` - List notifications (paginated, `?unread=true`)
121	- `PATCH /api/notifications/:id/read` - Mark read
122	- `POST /api/notifications/read-all` - Mark all read
123	- `POST /api/notifications/:id/retry` - Retry failed notification
124	- `GET /api/notifications/preferences` - Get preferences
125	- `PUT /api/notifications/preferences` - Update preferences
126	
127	### Analytics (administrator, compliance_analyst)
128	- `GET /api/analytics/funnel` - View-to-order funnel (`?days=7|30`)
129	- `GET /api/analytics/retention` - Retention cohorts (`?days=7|30`)
130	- `GET /api/analytics/content-performance` - Content performance (`?limit=20`)
131	
132	### A/B Tests (administrator)
133	- `POST /api/ab-tests` - Create test
134	- `GET /api/ab-tests` - List tests
135	- `GET /api/ab-tests/:id` - Get test with results
136	- `POST /api/ab-tests/:id/rollback` - Rollback test
137	- `GET /api/ab-tests/assignments` - Get user's variant assignments (all authenticated)
138	
139	### Admin (administrator)
140	- `GET /api/admin/ip-rules` - List IP rules
141	- `POST /api/admin/ip-rules` - Create IP rule
142	- `DELETE /api/admin/ip-rules/:id` - Delete IP rule
143	- `GET /api/admin/anomalies` - List anomaly alerts (administrator, compliance_analyst)
144	- `PATCH /api/admin/anomalies/:id/acknowledge` - Acknowledge anomaly
145	- `GET /api/admin/metrics` - System metrics
146	
147	## Order State Machine
148	
149	```
150	pending -> confirmed -> processing -> completed
151	  |           |
152	  v           v
153	cancelled  cancelled
154	```
155	
156	Valid transitions:
157	- `pending` -> `confirmed` or `cancelled`
158	- `confirmed` -> `processing` or `cancelled`
159	- `processing` -> `completed`
160	
161	## Security Features
162	
163	- **No default credentials** — the first admin must be created via a secure bootstrap endpoint; no passwords are embedded in migrations or committed to version control
164	- JWT authentication via HttpOnly cookies (15-min access, 7-day refresh with rotation)
165	- CSRF protection (double-submit cookie pattern)
166	- AES-256-GCM encryption for PII (emails)
167	- bcrypt password hashing (cost 12)
168	- Account lockout after 5 failed logins (30-minute cooldown)
169	- Per-endpoint rate limiting — user-based **and** IP-based (login, orders, messages, listings)
170	- CIDR-based IP allow/deny rules
171	- PII detection in messages (SSN, phone numbers)
172	- Refresh token family revocation (detects token reuse attacks)
173	- Append-only audit log for auth events, admin actions, order transitions, and moderation
174	- Encrypted keyfile secrets required in non-development environments (staging/production)
175	- Setup guard middleware blocks all API access until initial admin bootstrap is complete
176	
177	## Background Workers
178	
179	| Worker | Interval | Purpose |
180	|--------|----------|---------|
181	| Notification Retry | 60s | Retries failed notifications with exponential backoff |
182	| Anomaly Detector | 5min | Detects excessive cancellations and checkout failures |
183	| A/B Test Evaluator | 5min | Computes conversion rates, auto-rollback if threshold exceeded |
184	| Metrics Writer | 60s | Writes Prometheus-style metrics to log files |
185	| Analytics Rollup | 1h | Pre-aggregates funnel and retention metrics |
186	| Token Cleanup | 1h | Removes expired refresh tokens |
187	
188	## Running Tests
189	
190	```bash
191	./run_tests.sh
192	```
193	
194	This runs both unit tests and API tests. API tests require services to be running (`docker compose up`).
195	
196	- `unit_tests/` - Core logic tests (state transitions, validation, PII detection)
197	- `API_tests/` - Functional API tests (auth, CRUD, permissions, error handling)
198	
199	## Environment Variables
200	
201	| Variable | Default | Description |
202	|----------|---------|-------------|
203	| APP_ENV | (none) | Must be explicitly set to `development` for plaintext env secrets; all other values (including unset) require `SECRETS_KEYFILE` |
204	| DB_PASSWORD | changeme | PostgreSQL password |
205	| JWT_SIGNING_KEY | dev_jwt_secret_change_in_production | JWT signing key (development only) |
206	| AES_MASTER_KEY | (dev key) | 256-bit hex key for AES encryption (development only) |
207	| LISTEN_ADDR | :8080 | Backend listen address |
208	| DATABASE_URL | (auto-composed) | PostgreSQL connection string |
209	| SECRETS_KEYFILE | | Path to AES-256-GCM encrypted keyfile (required when APP_ENV != development) |
210	| SECRETS_PASSPHRASE | | Passphrase to decrypt the keyfile |
211	
212	## Project Structure
213	
214	```
215	.
216	├── docker-compose.yml       # Service orchestration
217	├── README.md                # This file
218	├── TEST_PROMPT.md           # Test verification document
219	├── run_tests.sh             # Test runner script
220	├── unit_tests/              # Unit tests
221	├── API_tests/               # API integration tests
222	├── backend/
223	│   ├── Dockerfile
224	│   ├── cmd/server/          # Entry point
225	│   ├── internal/
226	│   │   ├── cache/           # In-memory cache with TTL
227	│   │   ├── config/          # Environment configuration
228	│   │   ├── crypto/          # AES-256-GCM, bcrypt
229	│   │   ├── dto/             # Request/response types, errors
230	│   │   ├── handler/         # HTTP handlers
231	│   │   ├── middleware/      # Auth, CSRF, rate limiting, IP filter, logging
232	│   │   ├── model/           # Domain models
233	│   │   ├── router/          # Route definitions
234	│   │   ├── service/         # Business logic
235	│   │   ├── store/           # Database access (pgx)
236	│   │   └── worker/          # Background jobs
237	│   └── migrations/          # SQL migrations (auto-applied on startup)
238	├── frontend/
239	│   ├── Dockerfile
240	│   ├── nginx.conf           # Reverse proxy config
241	│   └── src/
242	│       ├── api/             # API client (axios)
243	│       ├── components/      # Layout + shared components
244	│       ├── pages/           # Route pages
245	│       ├── store/           # Zustand stores
246	│       ├── types/           # TypeScript types
247	│       └── utils/           # Formatters, PII detection
248	└── scripts/
249	    ├── seed.sql             # Sample data
250	    └── generate-keys.sh     # Key generation utility
251	```
252	
[result-id: r1]

1	# LedgerMint - Test Cases and Verification Steps
2	
3	This document contains all test cases and verification steps for validating the LedgerMint Digital Collectibles Exchange platform.
4	
5	---
6	
7	## 1. Infrastructure Verification
8	
9	### 1.1 Docker Compose Startup
10	- [ ] Run `docker compose up` from the repository root
11	- [ ] Verify all three services start: `postgres`, `backend`, `frontend`
12	- [ ] Verify PostgreSQL health check passes (backend depends on it)
13	- [ ] Verify backend starts and applies migrations (check logs for "applied migration")
14	- [ ] Verify frontend Nginx serves the app
15	
16	### 1.2 Service Accessibility
17	| Check | URL | Expected |
18	|-------|-----|----------|
19	| Frontend loads | http://localhost | Login page HTML with "LedgerMint" title |
20	| API responds | http://localhost:8080/api/auth/login | JSON error (method/body validation) |
21	| API via Nginx proxy | http://localhost/api/auth/login | Same JSON error via Nginx proxy |
22	| PostgreSQL accessible | `docker compose exec postgres psql -U ledgermint -c "SELECT 1"` | Returns 1 |
23	
24	### 1.3 Seed Data
25	- [ ] Run: `docker compose exec postgres psql -U ledgermint -d ledgermint -f /dev/stdin < scripts/seed.sql`
26	- [ ] Verify 4 users exist: `admin`, `seller1`, `buyer1`, `analyst1`
27	- [ ] Verify 3 collectibles exist with correct titles
28	
29	---
30	
31	## 2. Authentication Tests
32	
33	### 2.1 Login - Valid Credentials
34	| User | Password | Expected Status | Expected Roles |
35	|------|----------|----------------|----------------|
36	| admin | testpass123 | 200 | administrator |
37	| seller1 | testpass123 | 200 | seller |
38	| buyer1 | testpass123 | 200 | buyer |
39	| analyst1 | testpass123 | 200 | compliance_analyst |
40	
41	**Verify for each login:**
42	- Response contains `user.username` and `roles` array
43	- `access_token` cookie is set (HttpOnly)
44	- `refresh_token` cookie is set (HttpOnly, path=/api/auth)
45	- `csrf_token` cookie is set (NOT HttpOnly, readable by JavaScript)
46	
47	### 2.2 Login - Invalid Credentials
48	| Input | Expected Status | Expected Error Code |
49	|-------|----------------|-------------------|
50	| Wrong password | 401 | ERR_INVALID_CREDENTIALS |
51	| Nonexistent username | 401 | ERR_INVALID_CREDENTIALS |
52	| Username < 3 chars | 422 | ERR_VALIDATION |
53	| Password < 8 chars | 422 | ERR_VALIDATION |
54	| Missing password field | 422 | ERR_VALIDATION |
55	| Empty body | 400 | ERR_VALIDATION |
56	
57	### 2.3 Account Lockout
58	- [ ] Send 5 failed login attempts for the same user
59	- [ ] Verify 6th attempt returns HTTP 423 (ERR_ACCOUNT_LOCKED)
60	- [ ] Verify account auto-unlocks after 30 minutes (or admin unlock)
61	
62	### 2.4 Token Refresh
63	- [ ] After login, POST to `/api/auth/refresh` with refresh_token cookie
64	- [ ] Verify new access_token and refresh_token cookies are issued
65	- [ ] Verify refresh without cookie returns 401
66	
67	### 2.5 Logout
68	- [ ] POST to `/api/auth/logout` with valid auth
69	- [ ] Verify cookies are cleared (MaxAge=-1)
70	- [ ] Verify subsequent requests with old tokens return 401
71	
72	### 2.6 CSRF Protection
73	- [ ] POST to a protected endpoint without X-CSRF-Token header
74	- [ ] Verify request is rejected (403)
75	- [ ] Verify GET requests are not affected by CSRF
76	
77	---
78	
79	## 3. User Management Tests (Administrator Only)
80	
81	### 3.1 Create User
82	- [ ] POST `/api/users` as admin with valid data -> 201
83	- [ ] Verify response contains user ID, username, display_name
84	- [ ] POST `/api/users` as buyer -> 403 (permission denied)
85	- [ ] POST `/api/users` with duplicate username -> 409 (conflict)
86	- [ ] POST `/api/users` with missing required fields -> 422
87	
88	### 3.2 List Users
89	- [ ] GET `/api/users` as admin -> 200, paginated response
90	- [ ] Verify `data`, `page`, `page_size`, `total_count`, `total_pages` fields
91	- [ ] GET `/api/users` as buyer -> 403
92	
93	### 3.3 Role Management
94	- [ ] POST `/api/users/:id/roles` with `{"role_name":"buyer"}` -> 200/201
95	- [ ] Verify invalid role name returns 422
96	- [ ] DELETE `/api/users/:id/roles/:roleId` -> 200
97	
98	### 3.4 Account Unlock
99	- [ ] POST `/api/users/:id/unlock` as admin -> 200
100	- [ ] Verify locked user can log in again after unlock
101	
102	---
103	
104	## 4. Collectible Tests
105	
106	### 4.1 List Collectibles
107	- [ ] GET `/api/collectibles` -> 200, paginated
108	- [ ] Default status filter is "published"
109	- [ ] Query param `?status=hidden` returns hidden items (admin)
110	- [ ] Unauthenticated request -> 401
111	
112	### 4.2 Get Collectible
113	- [ ] GET `/api/collectibles/:id` -> 200
114	- [ ] Response contains `collectible` and `transaction_history`
115	- [ ] Invalid UUID -> 400
116	- [ ] Nonexistent ID -> 404
117	
118	### 4.3 Create Collectible (Seller Only)
119	- [ ] POST `/api/collectibles` as seller with valid data -> 201
120	- [ ] Verify auto-generated token_id and chain_id
121	- [ ] POST as buyer -> 403
122	- [ ] Missing title -> 422
123	- [ ] Price cents = 0 -> 422
124	- [ ] Price cents negative -> 422
125	
126	### 4.4 Update Collectible (Seller Only)
127	- [ ] PATCH `/api/collectibles/:id` as owner seller -> 200
128	- [ ] PATCH as different seller -> 403 (forbidden)
129	- [ ] Verify only provided fields are updated
130	
131	### 4.5 Moderation (Administrator Only)
132	- [ ] PATCH `/api/collectibles/:id/hide` with reason -> 200
133	- [ ] Verify collectible no longer appears in published list
134	- [ ] PATCH `/api/collectibles/:id/publish` -> 200
135	- [ ] Verify collectible reappears in published list
136	- [ ] Hide without reason -> 422
137	- [ ] Hide as seller -> 403
138	
139	### 4.6 Seller's Own Listings
140	- [ ] GET `/api/collectibles/mine` as seller -> 200
141	- [ ] GET `/api/collectibles/mine` as buyer -> 403
142	
143	---
144	
145	## 5. Order Tests
146	
147	### 5.1 Create Order (Buyer Only)
148	- [ ] POST `/api/orders` with valid collectible_id and Idempotency-Key -> 201
149	- [ ] Verify order status = "pending"
150	- [ ] Verify price_snapshot_cents matches collectible price
151	- [ ] POST without Idempotency-Key -> 400
152	- [ ] POST as seller -> 403
153	- [ ] POST for own collectible (seller also has buyer role) -> 422
154	
155	### 5.2 Idempotency
156	- [ ] Send same request with same Idempotency-Key twice
157	- [ ] Verify same order ID returned both times (no duplicate created)
158	
159	### 5.3 Overselling Prevention
160	- [ ] Create order for a collectible that already has an active order
161	- [ ] Verify 409 ERR_OVERSOLD response
162	
163	### 5.4 Order State Transitions
164	
165	**Happy path:**
166	| From | To | Actor | Endpoint | Expected |
167	|------|-----|-------|----------|----------|
168	| pending | confirmed | seller | POST /orders/:id/confirm | 200 |
169	| confirmed | processing | seller | POST /orders/:id/process | 200 |
170	| processing | completed | seller | POST /orders/:id/complete | 200 |
171	
172	**Cancellation:**
173	| From | Actor | Endpoint | Expected |
174	|------|-------|----------|----------|
175	| pending | buyer or seller | POST /orders/:id/cancel | 200 |
176	| confirmed | buyer or seller | POST /orders/:id/cancel | 200 |
177	
178	**Invalid transitions:**
179	| From | To | Expected |
180	|------|-----|----------|
181	| pending | processing | 422 ERR_INVALID_TRANSITION |
182	| pending | completed | 422 ERR_INVALID_TRANSITION |
183	| processing | cancelled | 422 ERR_INVALID_TRANSITION |
184	| completed | any | 422 ERR_INVALID_TRANSITION |
185	| cancelled | any | 422 ERR_INVALID_TRANSITION |
186	
187	**Permission errors:**
188	- [ ] Buyer cannot confirm/process/complete orders (403)
189	- [ ] Cancel requires reason field (422 without it)
190	
191	### 5.5 Fulfillment Tracking
192	- [ ] PATCH `/api/orders/:id/fulfillment` as seller with carrier/tracking_number -> 200
193	- [ ] Verify fulfillment_tracking JSON is stored on order
194	
195	### 5.6 List Orders
196	- [ ] GET `/api/orders?role=buyer` -> buyer's orders
197	- [ ] GET `/api/orders?role=seller` -> seller's orders
198	- [ ] Pagination works correctly
199	
200	---
201	
202	## 6. Message Tests
203	
204	### 6.1 Send Message
205	- [ ] POST `/api/orders/:orderId/messages` as buyer with body -> 201
206	- [ ] POST as seller of that order -> 201
207	- [ ] POST as unrelated user -> 403
208	- [ ] POST with empty body -> 422
209	
210	### 6.2 List Messages
211	- [ ] GET `/api/orders/:orderId/messages` -> 200, paginated
212	- [ ] Only buyer and seller of the order can list
213	
214	### 6.3 Attachment
215	- [ ] Send message with file attachment < 10MB -> 201
216	- [ ] Send message with file > 10MB -> 413
217	
218	### 6.4 PII Detection (Frontend)
219	- [ ] Enter SSN pattern (123-45-6789) in message box -> blocked with warning
220	- [ ] Enter phone number -> blocked with warning
221	- [ ] Normal text -> no warning, message sends
222	
223	---
224	
225	## 7. Notification Tests
226	
227	### 7.1 List Notifications
228	- [ ] GET `/api/notifications` -> 200, paginated
229	- [ ] `?unread=true` filters to unread only
230	
231	### 7.2 Mark Read
232	- [ ] PATCH `/api/notifications/:id/read` -> 200
233	- [ ] POST `/api/notifications/read-all` -> 200
234	- [ ] Verify unread count updates
235	
236	### 7.3 Retry Failed Notification
237	- [ ] POST `/api/notifications/:id/retry` -> 200
238	
239	### 7.4 Notification Preferences
240	- [ ] GET `/api/notifications/preferences` -> 200
241	- [ ] PUT with `{"preferences":{"order_confirmed":false}}` -> 200
242	- [ ] Verify preference persists on next GET
243	
244	### 7.5 Order Status Notifications
245	- [ ] Confirm an order -> buyer receives notification with template "order_confirmed"
246	- [ ] Complete an order -> buyer receives notification with template "order_completed"
247	- [ ] Cancel an order -> buyer receives notification with template "order_cancelled"
248	
249	---
250	
251	## 8. Analytics Tests (Administrator + Compliance Analyst)
252	
253	### 8.1 Funnel Analytics
254	- [ ] GET `/api/analytics/funnel?days=7` as admin -> 200
255	- [ ] Response contains `views`, `orders`, `rate`, `days`
256	- [ ] GET as buyer -> 403
257	
258	### 8.2 Retention Cohorts
259	- [ ] GET `/api/analytics/retention?days=30` -> 200
260	- [ ] Response is array of `{cohort_date, cohort_size, retained_count, retention_rate}`
261	
262	### 8.3 Content Performance
263	- [ ] GET `/api/analytics/content-performance?limit=10` -> 200
264	- [ ] Response contains collectible-level view/order/conversion data
265	
266	### 8.4 Compliance Analyst Access
267	- [ ] Login as `analyst1` (compliance_analyst role)
268	- [ ] Verify can access `/api/analytics/funnel` -> 200
269	- [ ] Verify can access `/api/admin/anomalies` -> 200
270	- [ ] Verify CANNOT access `/api/ab-tests` -> 403
271	- [ ] Verify CANNOT access `/api/users` -> 403
272	
273	---
274	
275	## 9. A/B Test Management (Administrator Only)
276	
277	### 9.1 CRUD Operations
278	- [ ] POST `/api/ab-tests` with valid data -> 201
279	- [ ] GET `/api/ab-tests` -> 200, array of tests

311	
312	## 11. Error Response Format
313	
314	All API errors must follow this structure:
315	```json
316	{
317	  "error": {
318	    "code": "ERR_<TYPE>",
319	    "message": "Human-readable message",
320	    "request_id": "uuid"
321	  }
322	}
323	```
324	
325	### Verify for each error type:
326	| HTTP Status | Error Code | Trigger |
327	|-------------|-----------|---------|
328	| 400 | ERR_VALIDATION | Invalid request body |
329	| 401 | ERR_UNAUTHORIZED | No auth cookie |
330	| 401 | ERR_INVALID_CREDENTIALS | Wrong password |
331	| 403 | ERR_FORBIDDEN | Insufficient role |
332	| 404 | ERR_NOT_FOUND | Resource doesn't exist |
333	| 409 | ERR_CONFLICT | Duplicate resource |
334	| 409 | ERR_OVERSOLD | Collectible has active order |
335	| 413 | ERR_ATTACHMENT_TOO_LARGE | Attachment > 10MB |
336	| 422 | ERR_VALIDATION | Failed validation |
337	| 422 | ERR_INVALID_TRANSITION | Invalid state change |
338	| 423 | ERR_ACCOUNT_LOCKED | Account locked |
339	| 429 | ERR_RATE_LIMITED | Too many requests |
340

438	## 13. Running Automated Tests
439	
440	```bash
441	# Run all tests
442	./run_tests.sh
443	
444	# Run only unit tests (no services needed)
445	./run_tests.sh unit
446	
447	# Run only API tests (services must be running)
448	./run_tests.sh api
449	```
450	
451	### Expected Results
452	- **Unit tests**: All tests in `unit_tests/` pass (state machine, PII detection, validation, formatters, error codes)
453	- **API tests**: All tests in `API_tests/` pass (auth, collectibles, orders, messages, notifications, admin)
454	- **Summary**: Clear PASS/FAIL counts printed at the end
